productDecision.decision: FLOW-19 Durable Sagas and Compliance is eligible to proceed into Docker/Vault auth and fork cascade certification after repairing inherited portability defects in the four flow services.

fixMap.rowSummary: FLOW-19 requires AI Protocol Phase 1 first, tenant context service decoupling as the second pilot, then data classification annotation, plugin SDK publication, and configuration key scoping before full distribution proof.
fixMap.firstTestable: Phase 1 now; template after tenant context service decoupling; distribution after data classification, plugin SDK publication, and configuration key scoping.

sources: docs/design-reviews/ROLE-ANALYSIS-BATCH-04.md FLOW-19 Durable Sagas and Compliance section

roles.use: tenant-scoped saga dashboard and compliance audit dashboard
cross-tenant saga operations, dead-letter queue, compensation-chain debug, and audit export
read-only saga state and compliance audit inspection for support tickets
roles.note: wrong-role case for the shared run trace probe if the probe permits tenant-user, tenant-admin, and platform-admin

notes: ROLE-ANALYSIS-BATCH-04 records 3 full FLOW-19 cells plus 1 partial tenant-user cell.

preExistingDefectCheck.rootCause: The four FLOW-19 production services still used the old direct ClsService tenant access pattern and did not extend MicroserviceBase. This matched the PER-FLOW-FIX-MAP row that marks FLOW-19 tenant context service decoupling as the second pilot.

fixApplied.summary: Replaced direct ClsService/TenantContext imports with TenantContextResolver, added MicroserviceBase inheritance and service descriptors, and renamed injected fabrics to dbFabric/queueFabric to avoid base-class getter collisions.

typescript.command: Push-Location server; npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json --pretty false; Pop-Location
